name: Serbian Empire (Nemanjić Dynasty)
note: Stefan Nemanja founded the medieval Serbian state (1168). His son Sava established the Serbian Orthodox Church (1219) — the key institution of Serbian national identity. Stefan Dušan (1331–1355) expanded Serbia to its greatest extent (Macedonia, Albania, Greece) and crowned himself Emperor of the Serbs and Greeks. The empire collapsed after his death; the Battle of Kosovo (1389) against the Ottomans ended Serbian independence for five centuries.

figures.name: Stefan Nemanja
figures.significance: Founded the Nemanjić dynasty and unified the Serbian tribes; abdicated to become a monk (Saint Simeon); his son Sava established the autocephalous Serbian Orthodox Church

figures.name: Stefan Dušan
figures.significance: The greatest Serbian medieval ruler; crowned Emperor of the Serbs and Greeks 1346; expanded Serbia across the Balkans; his legal code (Dušan's Code) remains foundational

figures.name: Lazar Hrebeljanović
figures.significance: Led the Serbian coalition at the Battle of Kosovo (1389) against the Ottomans; killed in battle; became a martyr saint of the Serbian Orthodox Church; Kosovo remains the most sacred site in Serbian national memory
